title: The Brew Shop - Weekly Email Update
contentHtml:
Hey Brew Shop Fam!
Here's an update of what's happening this week - from new beer drops, wine reviews, our current tap list, to upcoming tastings, and more. 

Join us this Friday from 5-7 PM as we welcome Robins Cellars for our free tasting! Scroll down to see our full tasting line-up for May.

🚨 New England bangers!
• Little Willow Night Sky DDH IPA with Paradisi & Citra
• Little Willow Hazy Daze DDH DIPA with Citra & Galaxy
• Trillium Congress Street IPA with Australian Galaxy
• Trillium Fort Point Pale Ale with Citra & Columbus
• Trillium Summer Ale with orange, pink peppercorn & black tea
• Trillium Mini Mallard Pale Ale with Nelson Sauvin & Strata

🎯 Little bit of everything from shop-favorite Fonta Flora
• Hop Quilt West Coast pale ale with Citra & Strata
• Mimosa City white ale with fresh oranges, earl grey tea & coriander
• Lightless Walk West Coast IPA with Columbus Cryo, Strata & Zamba - collab with TRVE
• Unusual Frequency Track 14 Hazy DIPA with Citra, Citra cryo & Motueka - collab with Charles Towne Fermentory
• Off Again Helles Lager brewed with hot granite rocks - collab with Cellarest

🙌 Allagash showing us how real sours are made
• Avance Barrel Aged Sour with Strawberries
• Coolship Cerise Barrel Aged Sour with Cherries

☄️ Fresh batch of hops from Widowmaker
• Blue Comet IPA with Simcoe & Comet
• Green Wizard West Coast IPA with Centennial & Cascade
• Dead Nebula Hazy DIPA with Galaxy & Citra Pellet Hops, Citra Cryo, & Galaxy Quantum
• Lightning From the Grave Hazy Pale Ale with Citra & Mosaic - collab with Coven

🌅 Manifesting those long summer nights with Halfway Crooks
• Vary Czech Pilsner
• 10P Cerne Pivo Dark Czech Lager
• Sanguine Belgian Pale Ale

🥭 Still manifesting summer weather with Sierra Nevada
• Mango Little Thing IPA
• Otra Vez Gose with lime & blue agave nectar

🍎 Keeping it sweet n' sour
• Old Towne Cidery Sweet Apple Cider
• Garden Path The Fruitful Can Barrel-Aged Sour fermented with boysenberries, blue haskap berries & blackberries

💅 Feeling absolutely diviiiiiiiiiine with Divine Barrel
• Canadian Lager
• Dank Zappa West Coast IPA with Citra, Mosaic & Idaho 7 hops
• Divine Funkin Nuggets West Coast IPA with Centennial, Amarillo, Riwaka, Motueka & Moutere - collab with 450 North

🫶 VA Hops
• The Answer Galactic Bounty Hazy IPA with Cryo Citra & Galaxy
• The Veil Broz Night Out3 TDH Hazy DIPA with Citra
• Crooked Run Verdant Force Hazy DIPA with Citra & Simcoe

🏔️ Freshies from Dutchess
• Volpe Italian Pilsner dry hopped with Spalter Select, Hallertau Mittelfrüh and Saphir
• Arcadia Pale Ale
• Urbier traditional Nürnberg-style Rotbier

🦓 Tripping Animals keeping busy with some fun collabs!
• Zenbara Rice Lager - collab with Japas
• Hounds vs Hydra Sour with raspberry, boysenberry, blackberry, cherry & marshmallow - collab with Mortalis
• Totality DDH West Coast IPA with Mosaic, Nectaron & Super Sauvin hops - collab with Everywhere
• Speckled Nectar DDH Hazy IPA with Citra Cryo, Nelson Cryo & Peachtree Hop Kief
• Benjamin the Elder Triple IPA with Lotus, Citra & Cashmere

🌴 Stay crispy
• Great Lakes Orange Honey Wheat
• Boulevard Tropical Tank 7 Saison
• Von Trapp Radler
• Thin Man Minkey Bluedle Sour with Blue Raspberry

🏄 New drops from Oxbow!
• Northern Lager
• Surf Casting Grisette with lime
• Izakaya Japanese Lager

🍹 Show some love for our Midwest friends
• Humble Forager Murky Maelstrom DIPA with Citra, Nectaron, Nelson Sauvin, & ZubZero Hop Kief - collab with Black Stack
• Humble Forager Enchanted Island Zombie Caribbean Cocktail Imperial Sour with passion fruit, pineapple, tangerine, cherry & lime
• Dovetail Summer Hopfenlager Pale Lager with Vista & Strata

Threes flexing their NYC muscle!

• Logical Conclusion Hazy IPA with Citra, Simcoe & Mosaic

• Gowanus Gold Rice Lager with Carolina Gold rice & NZ Pacifica hops

• Shapeless Days Summer Lager

• Luger Lager Bavarian Lager

• Silent Departure Irish Lager

Don't worry - there are still big 'ole stouts out there

• FIrestone Walker Parabola Bourbon Barrel Aged Russian Imperial Stout

• 3 Floyds Trolls Trolls Trolls Barrel Aged Imperial Stout with vanilla beans

Front porch sippin with Deciduous

• House Marg Unfiltered Pilsner with lime and salt

• Budburst TDH Hazy Pale Ale with Citra, Nectaron & Maui Nelson Hop Kief

• Perceived Value Double IPA with Citra, Nectaron & Motueka Hyperboost

🍃Lil something for our hop lovers from Cellarmaker

• The Glow Hazy IPA with Citra, Nelson Sauvin & Galaxy hops

• Pacific Crush West Coast IPA with Mosaic, Citra, NZ Cascade & Krush

• Permanent Daylight West Coast IPA with Nelson Sauvin, Citra & Centennial

• Wild Gooseberry Chase Hazy IPA with Nelson Sauvin

• Wizard Hand of Evil Hazy IPA with Mosaic, Nelson Sauvin, & Idaho 7

• Flying High West Coast DIPA with Simcoe, Strata, Krush & Columbus Cryo

*Expected Thursday*

New in from Old Rappahannock

• Pizza Pils Italian Pilsner

• Tight Lines Hazy IPA

🍋‍🟩 Lost Boy Cideritabringing the weekend sunshine!

Precarious Resin & Resolve West Coast IPA

Wild East L’Ultima Moda Italian Pils

New wines in the door!

Sohm & Kracher Lion Gruner Veltliner: Of all places, Aldo Sohm and Gerhard Kracher, two Austrians, met in New York in 2004. It was their love for dry gruner veltliners that sealed the friendship and a wine partnership. Organically grown and fermented with native yeast, Sohm & Kracher Gruner Veltliner is crisp and acidic with notes of fresh green apple, pear, and a squeeze of lemon-lime.

Brooks Pinot Blanc: Brooks affectionately refers to this wine as "Sunshine in a glass", and they are so right. Very aromatic, with notes of Honey-crisp apple, pear, and a lemony acidity. Expect a beautifully balanced texture from being 50% stainless and 50% neutral oak. The perfect spring and summer sipper, would pair wonderfully with shellfish. Organic, Native Yeast, Practicing Sustainable, Biodynamic.

Eric Cottat Rose Sancerre: We're big fans of Eric Cottat, so you can imagine our delight when we had the opportunity to grab his new vintage of Rose Sancerre. Eric is a one-man show who creates nuanced, crush-worthy Sancerre. We're loving his rendition of Rose Sancerre with a rose petal pink hue paired with floral aromas, crushed strawberry and chalky minerality.

It was their love for dry gruner veltliners that sealed the friendship and a wine partnership. We're loving his rendition of Rose Sancerre with a rose petal pink hue paired with floral aromas, crushed strawberry and chalky minerality.

Friends and neighbors (any fellow Jon Hamm fans?), 

With the dreariest summer kick off in recent history behind us, I think it’s time to manifest some warm and sunny days ahead. There’s only one appropriate way to do this here at Brew Shop: with a glass (or, let’s just be real ... 2 or 3… safe space, am I right?) of a crispy white wine! 

This week’s wine feature is dedicated to one of our newest arrivals, the Trebbiano d'Abruzzo from Coste di Brenta winery in Abruzzo, Italy. This was my first taste of the Trebbiano grape, and let’s just say… plane tickets have been perused (much to the chagrin of my practical husband!). But, while I fantasize about my Euro summer dreams, let’s take a (metaphorical, unfortunately) trip to central Italy, where the Abruzzo wine region sits between the Apennine mountains and the Adriatic sea. 

If you’ve been around the block (or specifically, around 2000 Wilson Blvd), I’m sure you’ve heard about the benefits of mountains and oceans for shaping the unique terroir of a wine producing region. High elevations provide intense sunlight exposure and dramatic temperature swings, while coastal proximity allows for cooling breezes and often passes on a distinct salinity to the grape skins. The harmony of these two forces–also seen in regions like Sonoma or Sicily–produce unique microclimates that yield robust reds and crisp whites. Enter the region’s flagship white grape: Trebbiano! Alongside the red superstar, Montepulciano d’Abruzzo, Trebbiano is the most planted grape in the region. Known for its light body and zesty acidity, this Trebbiano d’Abruzzo is beautifully textured, offering a delicate balance of stone fruit and floral aromas, with a subtle finish of salinity. Similar in their crisp, refreshing profiles and approachable price points, this grape offers a fun alternative for lovers of Pinot Grigio!

The winemakers behind this bottle are bragworthy in their own right. All of the wine from Coste di Brenta, a small family-run artisan winery, is hand-crafted and organically made. Established a little over two decades ago, the estate focuses entirely on organic farming and low-intervention, small-batch winemaking. The result? Unfussy wines that brilliantly showcase the unique attributes of the regional terroir.

She (yes, wines are always feminine, I don’t make the rules) would pair nicely with a variety of summer-friendly dishes. Akin to a fresh squeeze of lemon, the wine’s bright acidity would cut through the rich creaminess in pasta dishes like cacio e pepe or carbonara. For a more traditional pairing, drink this bottle alongside grilled fish or shellfish, where the minerality of the wine promises to complement and enhance the flavor of fresh seafood. But don’t worry! If you’ve hung up the pans and oven mitts for the night, throw together a quick charcuterie board featuring plenty of Italian cheeses, like pecorino or parmesan. The nutty aromas and rich salt profiles offer the perfect match for Trebbiano d’Abruzzo. 

This week’s writeup is brought to you by Mollie.

May Tasting Events are spring ready!

Thanks for your business! See you soon, Beth, Julie & Aidan and the entire Brew Crew
